title: CLI: Viewport Snapshot for Visual Regression Testing
description: Add a blender-cli snapshot command that captures a viewport render of a node group applied to a test mesh and saves it as an image file. During quilting development, numeric validation (vertex counts, Z ranges) caught structural errors but could not detect visual issues like 'the quilting looks flat on one face' or 'the stitching pattern is wrong.' A snapshot command would render the evaluated geometry from a specified camera angle (front, top, perspective, or custom) and save to a file. This enables visual regression testing: compare snapshots before and after changes to detect visual regressions that numeric metrics miss.
definition-of-done: 1. blender-cli snapshot --group Quilting --angle perspective --output quilting_test.png produces a viewport render. 2. Supports at least: front, top, right, perspective angles. 3. Accepts --mesh and --params flags. 4. Image is saved to the specified path. 5. Works headless (no GUI required).
testing-plan: 1. snapshot --group Quilting --angle perspective — produces a valid PNG. 2. snapshot --group Quilting --mesh cube --angle front — cube with quilting from front. 3. Verify the image clearly shows the quilting pattern and displacement.
todos: Set up camera at predefined angles (front, top, right, perspective)
Configure viewport shading for clear pattern visibility (solid mode with face orientation or matcap)
Render and save to specified output path
unknowns: Which viewport shading mode best reveals surface displacement? Matcap or solid with AO shows depth well. Wireframe overlay might help show the grid pattern.
Should this also support capturing the node tree layout as an image for documentation?
